action: Open Feather with MetaMask and another supported injected provider announced, choose MetaMask explicitly, approve one account, and compare the connected identity with MetaMask.
actor: A desktop visitor with multiple injected providers

expectedAssertions.statement: The app requires an explicit supported-provider choice and does not connect an unrelated provider.
The displayed full account and owner-scoped reads match the account selected in MetaMask.

negativePaths.trigger: Announce duplicate providers or change the selected account before connection completes.

prerequisites: MetaMask and a second injected provider are available.
recovery: Reload owner-scoped data after the account stabilizes.
title: Explicit provider and account connection

action: Exercise connection with no provider, a locked or unauthorized wallet, rejected permission, provider error, and duplicate announcements; restore a usable provider and retry.

expectedAssertions.statement: Each failure renders a truthful distinct message with an actionable next step and no false connected state.
Duplicate announcements are deduplicated or require explicit selection without enabling owner-dependent writes.

negativePaths.trigger: Reject permission, fail the provider request, or announce duplicate provider identities.

prerequisites: Provider fixtures cover missing, locked, unauthorized, rejected, errored, and duplicate states.
recovery: Install or unlock the selected wallet when required.
Use Connect again after restoring access.
title: Truthful provider failure handling

action: Connect on a nonconfigured chain, approve add and switch when required, then repeat with rejection, unsupported add-chain behavior, wallet mismatch, and an RPC reporting another chain.
actor: A connected wallet user changing networks

expectedAssertions.statement: Approved add or switch reaches the configured chain and both wallet and RPC chain identifiers are verified before writes.
Rejected, unsupported, wallet-mismatched, or RPC-mismatched states fail closed without simulation or wallet submission.

negativePaths.trigger: Reject switching or make the wallet and application RPC report different chains.

prerequisites: Configured chain parameters and wrong-chain fixtures are available.
recovery: Restore the configured RPC and refresh runtime state.
title: Chain add, switch, and mismatch handling

action: Connect account A, populate owner-scoped state including a submitted receipt and activity, disconnect, reconnect account A, then connect account B, reload, and inspect all owner-dependent views.
actor: A wallet user disconnecting and changing identity

expectedAssertions.statement: Disconnect clears executable balances, allowances, positions, drafts, and form terminal state from active views.
Reconnect loads the selected owner and exposes no prior-owner executable state while immutable records remain original-owner scoped.
Account B cannot see account A's owner-scoped receipts or activity before or after reload, while account A's immutable record remains retrievable only under account A.

negativePaths.trigger: Disconnect with a reviewed draft and submitted receipt visible, reconnect using a different account, and reload.

prerequisites: Accounts A and B have distinguishable state.
An unsubmitted draft and submitted record can be represented.
recovery: Wait for fresh owner reads before review.
Reload owner-scoped receipts and activity only for the selected account.
title: Disconnect and owner-state isolation

action: Prepare a reviewed unsubmitted write, change its account, chain, environment, or deployment context, then separately edit a new draft after handing the original intent to the wallet.
actor: A wallet user whose execution context changes

expectedAssertions.statement: A pre-handoff context change invalidates the reviewed fingerprint and prevents stale submission.
After handoff the original fingerprint and hash remain tracked under their original context while edits form a separate draft without implying cancellation.

negativePaths.trigger: Change a fingerprint field before handoff, or edit amount after the original request is pending.

prerequisites: A write flow exposes review and handoff boundaries.
recovery: Obtain a fresh review in the current context.
Track the original request independently of the new draft.
title: Context invalidation at wallet handoff

action: Open an exact pool deep link while disconnected, establish safe view state, connect the intended account, create a draft, reload and navigate history; repeat while a submitted transaction is pending and after its canonical receipt.
actor: A visitor or connected user navigating and reloading

expectedAssertions.statement: The exact pool identity and safe view state survive disconnected-to-connected transition, navigation, and reload without reviving executable quotes, drafts, or stale form banners.
Submitted and replacement hashes plus canonical receipt history persist under original context and reconcile without another wallet prompt.
After connection or reload, any executable draft requires fresh owner-scoped balances, allowance, quote, and preflight for the preserved exact pool.

negativePaths.trigger: Connect from a disconnected exact-pool view, then reload with an unsubmitted quote and a separate pending or replaced transaction.

prerequisites: A stable exact-pool deep link is available while disconnected.
Pending and replacement states can be represented.
recovery: Connect the intended account without changing the exact pool view.
Requote and preflight a fresh owner-scoped draft after connection or reload.
Resume persisted transaction reconciliation without resubmission.
title: Safe deep-link and transaction persistence

action: Connect an account with known ETH balance, review a buffered gas estimate, test token sufficiency with inadequate ETH, then change gas conditions before wallet acceptance.
actor: A wallet user funding transaction gas

expectedAssertions.statement: The interface identifies ETH as native gas currency and shows current native balance.
Insufficient gas is distinct from token insufficiency, blocks handoff, and estimate drift requires refreshed review rather than an exact-cost promise.

negativePaths.trigger: Provide enough token but inadequate ETH, or change the gas estimate after review.

prerequisites: Native balance and gas estimation are controllable.
recovery: Fund ETH or reduce native value where appropriate.
title: Native gas balance and drift recovery
